2605.10853.title: Grounded Satirical Generation with RAG
2605.10853.paper_summary: Humor generation remains challenging task for Large Language Models (LLMs), due to their subjective nature. We focus on satire, a form of humor strongly shaped by context. In this work, we present a novel pipeline for grounded satire generation that uses Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) over current news to produce satirical dictionary definitions in the Finnish context. We also introduce a new task-specific evaluation framework and annotate 100 generated definitions with six human annotators, enabling analysis across multiple experimental conditions, including cultural background, source-word type, and the presence or absence of RAG. Our results show that the generated definitions are perceived as more political than humorous. Both topic-based word selection and RAG improve the political relevance of the outputs, but neither yields clear gains in humor generation. In addition, our LLM-as-a-judge evaluation of five state-of-the-art models indicates that LLMs correlate well with human judgments on political relevance, but perform poorly on humor. We release our code and annotated dataset to support further research on grounded satire generation and evaluation.
2605.10853.paper_summary_zh: 幽默生成對於大型語言模型（LLMs）來說仍然是一項具有挑戰性的任務，因為其主觀性質。我們專注於諷刺，這是一種受上下文強烈影響的幽默形式。在這項工作中，我們提出了一個新穎的基於檢索增強生成（RAG）的諷刺生成流程，利用當前新聞在芬蘭語境中生成諷刺字典定義。我們還引入了一個新的任務特定評估框架，並由六位人類標註者對100個生成的定義進行標註，使得可以在多種實驗條件下進行分析，包括文化背景、來源詞類型以及RAG的存在或缺失。我們的結果顯示，生成的定義被認為更具政治性而非幽默性。基於主題的詞彙選擇和RAG提高了輸出的政治相關性，但兩者都未能在幽默生成上帶來明顯的提升。此外，我們對五個最先進模型進行的LLM作為評判者的評估顯示，LLMs與人類對政治相關性的判斷高度相關，但在幽默方面表現不佳。我們發布了代碼和標註數據集，以支持對基於上下文的諷刺生成和評估的進一步研究。
2605.10853.author: Oona Itkonen et.al.
2605.10853.authors: Oona Itkonen, Yuxin Su, Linyao Du, Ona De Gibert
